html:
Erik Eriksson, including variations such as Erik/Eric and Eriksson/Ericsson/Erikson/Ericson, may refer to:

  • Erik Eriksson (king) (1216–1250): Swedish king, also known as "Erik the Scholar" and "Erik the Lame."
  • Erik Eriksson (Gyllenstierna), senior (born in the 1470s): Swedish lawman and military officer.
  • Erik Eriksson (Gyllenstierna), junior (died 1502): Swedish knight and commander of Älfsborg fortress.
  • Erik Eriksson (surveyor) in 17th-century Västerbotten.
  • Erick Ericksson (1695–1761): Radical Pietist, see Skevikarna.
  • Erik Eriksson (1718–1769): Mayor and member of parliament.
  • Eric Ericsson (1763–1828): Member of parliament and industrialist.
  • Eric Ericsson in Ruda (1787–1863): Swedish large-scale farmer from Östergötland.
  • Eric Erickson (spy) (1890–1983): Swedish businessman and spy.
  • Eric Erickson (1892–1965): Swedish-American baseball player.
  • Erik H. Erikson (1902–1994): Jewish-American psychoanalyst.
  • Erik Eriksson (competitive cyclist) (1905–1999): Competitive cyclist.
  • Erik "Saxjerker" Eriksson (1906–1962): Swedish jazz musician.
  • Erik Eriksson (hydrologist) (1917–2019): Swedish hydrologist.
  • Eric Ericson (conductor) (1918–2013): Swedish choir conductor.
  • Erik Eriksson (filmmaker) (1933–2005): Swedish documentary filmmaker.
  • Erik Eriksson (author) (1937–2021): Swedish journalist and author.
  • Eric Ericson (author) (born 1972): Swedish author and designer.
  • Eric Ericson (actor) (born 1974): Swedish actor.
  • Erick Erickson (born 1975): American conservative blogger and TV commentator.
  • Erik Eriksson (cross-country skier): Swedish cross-country skier, grandson of Nils "Mora-Nisse" Karlsson.
  • Erik Eriksson (triple jumper) (born 1980): Swedish triple jumper.
  • Erik Eriksson (discus thrower): Swedish discus thrower, from the 1890s.

== Swedish Politicians == Erik Eriksson of Klaxås (1823–1891): Member of parliament, farmer. Erik Eriksson of Kväcklingen (1857–1936): Liberal politician, farmer. Erik Eriksson of Spraxkya (1864–1939): Chairman of the Farmers' League from 1916 to 1920. Erik Eriksson of Böleå (1866–1948): Liberal member of parliament, farmer. Eric Ericsson (right-wing politician) (1882–1962): Moderate member of parliament, ship captain and director. Eric Ericson (social democrat) (1888–1970): Member of parliament, mayor of Karlskoga. Ollas Erik Eriksson (1872–1953): Liberal member of parliament, farmer. Erik A. Eriksson (born 1969): Centre Party politician.

== See also == Ericus Erici Karl-Erik Eriksson

How certain are we about this?

For people from the Middle Ages, there are often contemporary written sources (letters, chronicles, church documents), so the main points are usually well-documented. However, these sources were mostly written by the Church and those in power, and their preservation is uneven, which can distort and limit our understanding.
tldr: The following list includes various individuals throughout history who share the name Erik Eriksson. Due to the commonality of this name, it encompasses a diverse range of people, from kings and politicians to athletes and artists.
lattlast:
Because the name Erik Eriksson is very common, there are many different individuals throughout history who have borne that name. The text illustrates this by mentioning people ranging from a Swedish king from the 13th century to modern athletes and actors.

Among those with this name are also several politicians who have served in the Riksdag, which is Sweden's parliament and highest legislative body. There are also individuals who have worked in the fields of art, music, and science.

The text also explains that it can be difficult to know everything about people who lived long ago. For those from earlier periods, letters and documents often exist from the church or authorities, but these are not always completely preserved.
